title: Apartments for rent in Maple Heights, OH
metaTitle: Apartments for Rent in Maple Heights, OH
metaDescription: Searching for apartments for rent in Maple Heights, Ohio? See rent ranges, transit, and Section 8 voucher facts, then tell our Cleveland team what you need.
primaryKeyword: apartments for rent Maple Heights ohio
answerBox: Maple Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb southeast/south of downtown Cleveland, near Garfield Heights. Cleveland-area apartment rents generally span roughly $1,060 to $1,350 a month across studio to three-bedroom units (Zumper, July 2026). Maple Heights does not currently have a local ordinance protecting Housing Choice Voucher holders.

sections.h2: Renting an apartment in Maple Heights
body: Maple Heights is one of Cuyahoga County's 59 municipalities, located southeast/south of downtown Cleveland alongside neighboring Garfield Heights in that same corridor of the county. As a separate city from Cleveland, it sets its own local ordinances even though it shares the same regional transit system and housing authority as the rest of the county.

For renters weighing a Cuyahoga County suburb against the city itself, the practical differences usually come down to local ordinances, which utility provider serves a given address, and whether a landlord accepts a Housing Choice Voucher — all covered in detail below.

Rent Finder Cleveland's current selection of 90+ Section 8-friendly rental homes is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods — Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker — rather than in Maple Heights specifically. [Tell our team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll flag relevant options as they come up, including nearby Cleveland listings that could work for your commute.

sections.h2: How much does an apartment cost near Maple Heights?
body: There isn't a Maple Heights-specific rent index in our sourcing, but the broader Cleveland-area apartment market gives useful context. Per Zumper's median-asking-rent report dated July 4, 2026, rents run from $1,060 for a studio up to $1,350 for a three-bedroom. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on professionally managed apartment communities shows meaningfully higher averages, since it tracks newer, larger buildings with more amenities rather than the full range of smaller, older listings.

Cleveland-area rent overall remains well below the national picture — Zumper estimates the metro runs about 35.9% cheaper than the U.S. median of $1,950 a month — a gap that generally carries over into the surrounding Cuyahoga County suburbs, though a specific Maple Heights unit's rent should always be confirmed directly with the landlord or building.

Greater Cleveland RTA (GCRTA) provides the region's public bus service across Cleveland and the Cuyahoga County suburbs, and Tower City in downtown Cleveland is the central hub where the Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront rail lines converge. Renters commuting from Maple Heights toward Downtown, the Cleveland Clinic, or University Circle generally combine RTA bus routes with the regional highway network rather than a direct rail connection.

Maple Heights sits in the higher-elevation southeastern part of the county, an area that typically sees more lake-effect snow accumulation each winter — roughly November through January — than Cleveland's western, lower-elevation lakefront neighborhoods.

Healthcare anchors the regional economy: Cleveland Clinic is Northeast Ohio's largest employer and University Hospitals ranks second in Cuyahoga County, so a commute to either system is worth mapping out before signing a lease in this part of the county.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers and apartments in Maple Heights
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, and Maple Heights does not currently have a local ordinance banning source-of-income denial. Whether a specific Maple Heights landlord accepts a voucher is up to that landlord.

A handful of nearby Cuyahoga County suburbs — Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, and Warrensville Heights among them — have passed local protections for voucher holders, per Signal Cleveland's 2024 reporting. Renters can submit a preliminary Housing Choice Voucher application year-round through [CMHA (Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php), the public housing authority serving the county, regardless of which municipality they eventually rent in.

Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Section 8 vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — our strongest and fully honest differentiator — though current listings sit in Cleveland proper rather than Maple Heights. Read our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full program breakdown, or check [Cleveland houses for rent](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) in the neighborhoods where we currently work with inventory.

CMHA's income limits for the voucher program, effective April 3, 2025, are $34,800 for a one-person household and $49,700 for a four-person household; these limits are updated annually, so confirm the current figures directly with CMHA before applying.

sections.h2: What to know before signing a lease near Maple Heights
body: Ohio Revised Code 5321.16 applies statewide: a landlord must return a tenant's security deposit within 30 days of move-out along with an itemized list of any deductions, and any deposit exceeding $50 or one month's rent (whichever is greater) accrues 5% annual interest if the tenant stays at least six months. Ohio also has no statewide rent control.

If you'd rather focus your search on areas where Rent Finder Cleveland currently works with Section 8-friendly rental homes, [browse our Cleveland apartment listings](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) or [book a free showing](/p/book-showing) to see current availability.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with apartments in Maple Heights?
faq.a: Our current selection is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods rather than Maple Heights itself. Tell our team what you're looking for and we'll point you to relevant options as they come up, including nearby Cleveland listings that might suit your commute.

faq.q: How much does an apartment cost near Maple Heights?
faq.a: We don't have Maple Heights-specific listing data, but the broader Cleveland-area market runs from about $1,060 for a studio to $1,350 for a three-bedroom, per Zumper's July 2026 report. Larger, professionally managed buildings price higher, per RentCafe's same-month data — always confirm a specific listing's asking rent directly with the building or landlord before applying.

faq.q: Are landlords in Maple Heights required to accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: No. Ohio has no statewide requirement, and Maple Heights has no local ordinance protecting voucher holders as of 2026, so acceptance is up to each landlord. Nearby Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, and Warrensville Heights do have such local protections.

faq.q: How do I apply for a Housing Choice Voucher near Maple Heights?
faq.a: Submit a preliminary application year-round through CMHA, the Cuyahoga County public housing authority, which serves the whole county regardless of which suburb you eventually rent in. Selection uses periodic random mini-lotteries rather than first-come, first-served order, so wait times range anywhere from a few days to several months depending on the lottery.

faq.q: What county is Maple Heights in?
faq.a: Maple Heights is one of Cuyahoga County's 59 municipalities, located southeast/south of downtown Cleveland next to Garfield Heights. It is a separate city from Cleveland with its own ordinances, utility arrangements, and Section 8 source-of-income rules, so renters should confirm the specifics for Maple Heights rather than assuming Cleveland's rules apply.
